title: 'Seize the day' — Machu Picchu trip prep, Aug 31 2021
html:
TL;DR

  • Apple Note from Aug 31, 2021 (Yahir age 18, ~one week into Peru) titled "-seize the day" — six bullets: Machu Picchu tickets, exchange currency, "other option for other activities", laundry, mouthwash, towel, "poncho-beanie setup".
  • Read against the Aug 21 packing list this is the fill-in-the-gaps note — Yahir is finalizing what's missing for the Peru leg: poncho + beanie for altitude. The "poncho-beanie setup" is the machu picchu-specific clothing that you can't really pack from Grants Pass in August.
  • "Other option for other activities" is the load-bearing line — it's the always have a plan B discipline showing up in the wild. Peru-Yahir is already running a contingency tree even on a tourist itinerary. Same instinct that later structures the Ha Giang loop and the six month adventure planning.
  • The title "seize the day" (carpe diem) is the most explicit manifesto style note framing of any 2021 trip note — the Peru leg is being lived as a deliberately seized event, not just a vacation. This pre-figures the six month adventure creed by three years.
  • Companion notes: 2021 08 21 pre peru packing list, 2021 08 24 jann people list (and the raw-only 2021 08 23 call lab for official covid test documentation COVID-test logistics) — together these notes cover the full pre-departure → in-country pivot week.

Key fragments

"-Machu Picchu tickets / -exchange currency / -other option for other activities / -poncho-beanie setup"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Densifies peru prep 2021 with the on-the-ground execution checklist (the cluster previously had departure logistics but not the in-country filler list).
  • Earliest carpe-diem-as-explicit-frame in the corpus — predates the voluntary adversity vocabulary by ~14 months.

Privacy

  • No PII in source.

Wikilinks
